family: Fries-, Photo-Fries, and Anionic Ortho-Fries Rearrangement
rationale: The Fries rearrangement of phenyl acetate to 2-hydroxyacetophenone is a classic, simple, and unambiguous textbook example of this reaction family. It involves the Lewis acid-catalyzed rearrangement of a phenolic ester to a hydroxy aryl ketone, which is the core transformation. The reactants and products are small, acyclic where possible, and easily parsed by RDKit.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses a simple phenyl group and an acetyl group, avoiding more complex or reactive functionalities. It is distinct from Claisen rearrangements (which involve allyl ethers), Friedel-Crafts acylations (which typically use an acyl halide and an arene as separate reactants), and other phenolic rearrangements, thus minimizing potential collisions with other named reaction benchmarks.
